inconsistent_layer_counts.recommendation: Update COMPLETE-LAYER-CATALOG.md with latest layer inventory

api_layers.description: Complete audit trail of every agent action, decision, and outcome

Post-remediation verification results — validates whether remediation (L68) successfully resolved breach (L67). Compares pre/post metrics, determines if SLO is now compliant, and feeds learning feedback (L57) for continuous improvement.

Service invocation requests — demand intake for work factory services (L62). Each request represents a demand for a service to be executed, tracks requester, priority, input payload, and request lifecycle. Fulfilled via service runs (L64).

Remediation plans for SLA breaches (L67) — step-by-step recovery procedures with resource estimates. Created automatically from runbooks or manually by operators. Feeds work execution units (L52) for automated remediation.

Investment decisions and business justification for new capabilities or service enhancements. Tracks business cases, ROI calculations, approval workflows, funding allocation, and actual returns. Enables investment portfolio management and budget planning.

Aggregate service performance metrics — computed view tracking success rates, timing, and resource consumption per service (L62). Derived from service runs (L64). Used for performance monitoring, capacity planning, and SLO compliance checking (L66, L67).

Service lifecycle events — tracks major transitions for work factory services (L62): deployment, upgrades, scaling, maintenance, deprecation, retirement. Provides audit trail for service evolution and operational history.
Impact analysis and effectiveness metrics per remediation execution
Automated remediation policies defining triggers, actions, and approval workflows

Service runtime execution instances — child records of service requests (L63). Each run represents an actual execution attempt of a service, with timing, status, output, and error tracking. Feeds performance profiles (L65) and SLO breach detection (L67).
Cloud cost tracking, budget alerts, and cost optimization

SLA breach records — incidents where service performance (L65) violates defined SLOs (L66). Triggers automated remediation planning (L68) and alerting. Complete self-healing loop: breach -> plan -> remediate -> revalidate.

Aggregate pattern performance metrics — computed view layer tracking how well each reusable pattern (L58) performs across all applications (L59). Provides success rates, timing data, and common adaptations for pattern tuning and selection guidance. Parent: None (aggregate/view layer, RESTRICT on pattern delete).

Pattern usage tracking — records each instance of a reusable pattern (L58) being applied to a work unit (L52). Tracks adaptations made, success score, and feedback for continuous improvement. Parent: work_execution_units (CASCADE on delete).

Service packaging of capabilities (L61). Each service is a concrete, invocable implementation that delivers one or more capabilities. Services are registered by agents/cp_agents and can be invoked via service requests (L63). Services have defined SLAs (L66) and performance tracking (L65).

Strategic roadmaps for capability and service evolution. Defines forward-looking initiatives, milestones, dependencies, and timelines for developing new capabilities or enhancing existing services. Enables strategic planning and capability gap analysis.

Portfolio management view of all work services. Provides executive-level oversight with service inventory, health rollups, capacity overview, and strategic prioritization. Enables portfolio-level reporting and resource allocation decisions.

Capability catalog backed by patterns (L58). Each capability represents a cohesive automation capability (e.g., 'schema migration automation', 'API deployment', 'test generation') that can be packaged into services (L62). Capabilities are abstract/compositional, services are concrete implementations.

Governance policies and compliance rules for the work factory. Defines policy rules, compliance requirements, approval thresholds, audit procedures, and enforcement mechanisms. Enables policy-driven governance, compliance auditing, and automated enforcement.

Service Level Objective (SLO) definitions and thresholds per service (L62). Defines expected performance, availability, and quality metrics. Breaches feed L67 work_service_breaches for automated remediation.
Infrastructure drift detection showing desired vs actual state
Historical performance analysis and trend detection

Factory-level KPIs and aggregate health metrics. Provides executive dashboard data with aggregate metrics across all portfolios and services, trend analyses, benchmarks, and target governance. Enables strategic decision-making and performance monitoring.
EVA data model meta-information describing all 51 layers and their relationships
Complete deployment history, changelogs, and rollback tracking
Multi-dimensional quality assessment for deployments
Real-time agent performance indicators and reliability metrics
Audit trail of all automated remediation executions with outcomes and evidence
Azure resource inventory, configuration, and state tracking
Compliance audit results, security findings, and remediation tracking
A FastAPI agent in the agent-fleet orchestrator
A reusable React component in the EVA design system (@eva/ui or screen-local)
A Cosmos DB container — fields, partition key, indexes
An ADR (Architecture Decision Record) for any significant technical or governance decision.
A single HTTP operation in the EVA API surface

Proof-of-completion for a DPDCA phase of a story. Universal schema works for any story type (feature, bug, infrastructure, etc) and any project. Session 27: Added tech_stack polymorphism for tech-specific context validation.
A named capability gate, maps to FeatureID enum in eva-brain-api
A custom React hook in the EVA codebase
Red-team vulnerability record from Promptfoo tests. Maps to attack tactics, frameworks, and remediation guidance.
Test suite performance metrics: pass/fail rates, coverage by framework, API costs, execution time.

Library of custom assertion definitions for red-team testing (Project 36). Assertions are reusable validation rules that can be applied to LLM outputs, API responses, and generated artifacts. Supports polymorphic assertion types (bilingual, pii-presence, latency-threshold, etc.).
OWASP + ATLAS + NIST attack taxonomy for red-teaming and LLM vulnerability testing. Maps attack tactics across security frameworks.
Framework control → CVE → remediation linker. Maps findings to compliance requirements and tracks gap resolution.
Individual CVE with CVSS score, affected assets, exploitability, and threat intel. Links to vulnerability scans.
Maps test executions to security control evidence. Links red-team findings to framework compliance status.
Promptfoo test pack definition with template metadata, assertion rules, and framework coverage settings.
Aggregate system-wide effectiveness metrics and continuous improvement insights
Prioritized fix action with SLA tracking, status, and effort estimation. Links CVE findings to remediation work.
Pareto-ranked vulnerabilities using 80/20 principle. Top 20% of CVEs account for ~80% of exploitability risk.
Global registry for all 163 screens across data-model layers, eva-faces, projects, and ops
CVE enrichment with exploit availability, active exploitation, trending data. Links threat intel to vulnerabilities.
Network/infrastructure scan execution record. Captures scan metadata, scope, timing, and result summary.
Enhanced project metadata with governance fields. Layer 25 in the EVA data model architecture.
Active work tracking for projects. Replaces STATUS.md with queryable, versioned work sessions. Layer 34 in the EVA data model architecture.
Runtime decision ledger. Captures decisions made during work execution (not architectural ADRs which are in L30 decisions layer). Layer 54 in the EVA data model architecture.
Operational work ledger. Each record represents a governed unit of work (e.g., implement feature, deploy service, fix bug, execute script). Layer 52 in the EVA data model architecture.
Result ledger. Records what was actually delivered vs what was expected. Layer 56 in the EVA data model architecture.
Execution event stream. Each record is one step in the timeline of a work unit (state transition, action taken, gate checked). Layer 53 in the EVA data model architecture.
Workspace-level configuration including best practices, bootstrap rules, and global settings. Layer 33 in the EVA data model architecture.
Adaptive learning layer capturing lessons, tuning signals, and improvement insights from work execution. Feeds into pattern creation (L58) and continuous improvement cycles.
Follow-up obligations created from work decisions (L54) and policy enforcement (L16). Tracks remediation tasks, policy-mandated actions, and decision follow-ups with assignees and due dates.

Approved execution pattern templates derived from learning feedback (L57). Each pattern defines a reusable workflow with steps, conditions, and expected outcomes. Forms the foundation for factory capabilities (L61).
An Azure resource or configuration object used by the EVA ecosystem
A UI string key with translations — every displayed string in admin-face and chat-face
A Model Context Protocol server that exposes tools to Copilot agents
A waterfall phase gate or governance checkpoint. Links to deliverables and sign-off evidence.
An actor (human role or machine agent) that interacts with EVA
A Prompty or system-prompt template used by agents and copilot skills
An Epic or requirement — linked to endpoints, screens, and tests for traceability
A project risk register entry. Supports both waterfall and agile risk tracking.
A React page/screen in admin-face or chat-face
A security control mapped from a governance framework (ITSG-33, OWASP LLM, MITRE ATLAS, NIST AI RMF)
A deployable service in the EVA ecosystem
An agile sprint (timebox) for a project. Tracks planned vs actual velocity and story completion.
End-to-end trace of LM calls from a single correlation_id. Captures model, tokens, cost, and latency per call. Partition key for Cosmos: correlation_id.
A TypeScript interface or type alias in the EVA codebase
Programme decomposition hierarchy: Program → Stream → Project → Epic → Feature → Story. Session 27: Layer 26 (L26) created.
